title: ORDERING LUNCH TO GO
conversation: Tim: Welcome to Angelo’s. What can I get for you? Joe: A chicken salad sandwich. Tim: For here or to go? Joe: To go. Tim: Would you like that on white, wheat, or pumpernickel? Joe: What’s pumpernickel? Tim: It’s a dark brown bread, similar to rye bread. Joe: Let me try that. Tim: And would you like that with mustard, mayonnaise, or oil? Joe: Mustard. But please go light on it. Tim: Would you like to make that a meal deal? Our special this month is a sandwich, an order of French fries, and a large soda for $6.99. Joe: I’m going to pass on that. But I’d like a side order of fries. Tim: Your total comes to $6.99. Joe: On second thought, I will take you up on that meal deal. Tim: Sure, then you’ll get a soda at no extra charge. That’ll be $6.99. Joe: Sorry, but I’ve only got a $100 bill. Tim: No worries. We can break it.

idioms.meaning_en: to use a small amount of something
idioms.meaning_vi: dùng ít, hạn chế dùng cái gì đó
idioms.example: Please go light on the salt when you cook the soup.
idioms.context: Mustard. But please go light on it.

idioms.meaning_en: a combination of food items sold together at a lower price than if bought separately
idioms.meaning_vi: suất ăn trọn gói, combo món ăn giá rẻ
idioms.example: The burger shop has a great meal deal that includes a drink and fries.
idioms.context: Would you like to make that a meal deal?

idioms.meaning_en: to decline an offer or opportunity
idioms.meaning_vi: từ chối một lời đề nghị
idioms.example: I think I'll pass on the invitation to the party tonight; I'm too tired.
idioms.context: I’m going to pass on that.

idioms.meaning_en: an extra dish served with the main course
idioms.example: I'd like a side order of onion rings with my steak.
idioms.context: But I’d like a side order of fries.

idioms.meaning_en: to reach a total amount or cost
idioms.meaning_vi: tổng cộng là, lên đến mức
idioms.example: The repair bill comes to two hundred dollars.
I was going to buy the red shirt, but on second thought, I'll take the blue one.
idioms.context: On second thought, I will take you up on that meal deal.

idioms.meaning_en: to accept an offer that someone has made
idioms.meaning_vi: chấp nhận lời đề nghị của ai đó
idioms.example: I’ll take you up on your offer to drive me to the airport.
idioms.context: I will take you up on that meal deal.

idioms.meaning_en: at no additional cost; for free
idioms.meaning_vi: không tính thêm phí, miễn phí thêm
idioms.example: They will deliver the furniture to your house at no extra charge.
idioms.context: Sure, then you’ll get a soda at no extra charge.

idioms.meaning_en: don't worry about it; it's okay
idioms.example: No worries, I can finish the report for you.

idioms.meaning_en: to change a large banknote for smaller bills or coins
idioms.meaning_vi: đổi tiền lẻ, phá tiền chẵn
idioms.example: I need to buy a ticket; can you break a fifty-dollar bill?
